title: alnus japonica var. borealis
intro: Alnus japonica var. borealis, also known as the Japanese green alder or mountain alder, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia, characterized by its slender branches and small, dark green leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Alnus japonica var. borealis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Alnus japonica var. borealis is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and is also used in traditional folk remedies.
